Preserve original WAV format on save

Save and export now write back in the original format (PCM 16/24-bit
or IEEE float 32-bit, mono or stereo) instead of always converting to
stereo 16-bit PCM.

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Fix false positives in exploit/scan modules (FW3.7.1)

- Add _discover_params(): crawl real page params before testing
- SQLi: use baseline comparison to filter pre-existing SQL-like text
- SQLi: time-based threshold now requires +4s vs baseline (not raw 2.5s)
- XSS: use unique session marker instead of generic payloads
- XSS: remove DOM sink false positives (innerHTML/eval exist everywhere)
- CMDi: use echo CMDI_CONFIRMED marker, remove generic 'ttl' indicator
- LFI: remove empty indicator payload (matched everything)
- LFI: use specific indicators (root:x:0:0:, HTTP_HOST=)
- vBulletin: require 'vbulletin' in response body (not just 302)
- upload_check: only report 200 with upload form content (not 403)
- sqli_scan: discover real params from page before injecting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: webgate
repository.description: webgate fw by c3less

commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: gateway-level JWT auth with same-origin proxy, eliminate CORS

Replace cross-origin Supabase function calls with same-origin proxy via
Vercel rewrites (prod) and Vite proxy (dev). JWT verification stays at
Supabase gateway; functions only decode payload for user identity.

- Add /api/functions/* reverse proxy in vercel.json and vite.config.ts
- Create getUserFromJWT() helper to extract user claims from gateway-verified JWT
- Remove CORS handling (cors.ts deleted, OPTIONS handlers removed)
- Switch 5 auth functions from user-scoped client to admin client + JWT decode
- Add verify_jwt=false config for stripe-webhook and admin-generate-codes

commit.message:
docs: add macOS SIP warning for cron in getting-started

Cron is not recommended on macOS due to System Integrity Protection
restricting access to user binaries, Homebrew paths, and keychain.
launchd (Option A) should be used instead.

commit.message:
fix: correct Codex app-server JSON-RPC protocol for thread/turn lifecycle

- thread/start must NOT include user message (was wrongly sent as "instructions")
- After thread/start response, send turn/start with the actual user message
- turn/start uses "input" array format [{type:"text",text:"..."}] not "message" string
- Fix notification handlers: use item/completed + item/started (not item/agentMessage/completed)
- Fix approval handling: respond with JSON-RPC response (same id) not serverRequest/resolved
- Properly distinguish server requests (id+method) from notifications (method only)
- Add reasoning/plan delta forwarding for reasoning models
- Add diagnostic logging for message flow tracing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: restart session in-place instead of leave+create

Added RestartSession RPC that resets game state while keeping the same
session and players. Solo mode auto-readies after restart. Multiplayer
keeps all players in the lobby to re-ready together.

- Proto: new RestartSession RPC on SessionService
- Server: reset board_state, turn_number, player scores/ready
- JS: handleRestartSession in ports.js, restartSession in grpc-client
- Elm: RestartSoloGame and RematchMultiplayer use restartSession

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix locale-specific date separators in DateTimeFormat (#2319)

toLocaleDateString was using hardcoded "/" separator for all numeric
date formats. Now derives both date component order and separator from
the locale's ShortDatePattern via DateTimeFormatInfo, fixing output for
locales like nl-NL (should use "-"), de-DE ("."), sv-SE ("-"), etc.

Also uses useUserOverride: false when creating CultureInfo to ensure
consistent behavior regardless of Windows regional settings.

Fixes #557

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(Option): Add per-item Disabled support to SelectInput options (#2496)

- Add `Disabled` property to `Option<T>` class with constructor parameter
- Add fluent `.Disabled()` extension method for `Option<T>`
- Update frontend to support disabled options in all SelectInput variants:
  - Select (dropdown) variant
  - List (checkbox) variant
  - Toggle variant
  - Radio variant
  - MultiSelect variant

- Update SelectInput.md documentation with new "Disabled Options" section
- Add SelectInputDisabledOptionsExample to example app

Closes #2361

commit.message:
Add VWAP reclaim proximity guard (0.5% max distance)

NVDA alert fired at $182.34 when VWAP was $179.87 (1.4% away) — too late.
Now skips if price is more than 0.5% above VWAP at fire time, catching
reclaims near the crossover instead of after the move already happened.

commit.message:
feat: MockBLEAdapter multi-mode telemetry and mode switching (#16)

* feat: multi-mode telemetry and mode switching for MockBLEAdapter

Add support for all 7 training modes (Weight, Resistance Band, Rowing,
Damper, Custom Curves, Isokinetic, Isometric) with mode-specific
kinematics profiles.

Add mode switching via setTrainingMode() public API and write() command
detection. Telemetry transitions on next tick with phase state reset and
mode confirmation notification.

Covers SDK-02.01 and SDK-02.02.
207 → 213 tests (15 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: extract KinematicsValues type and standardProfile factory

Address PR review feedback:
- Extract KinematicsValues interface for {position, velocity, force}
- Create ModeConstants + standardProfile() factory for parameterized modes
- 4 standard modes (weight, band, rowing, custom) use shared builder
- 3 special modes (damper, isokinetic, isometric) keep custom builders

Net -50 lines. All 213 tests pass unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: modularize MockBLEAdapter into focused modules

Split 500-line monolith into 5 files:
- mock.ts (167 lines) — adapter class, lifecycle, telemetry loop
- mock/types.ts (65 lines) — config, interfaces, defaults
- mock/kinematics.ts (129 lines) — physics engines per mode
- mock/profiles.ts (117 lines) — mode-to-profile mapping
- mock/notifications.ts (61 lines) — pure notification builders

Notification builders are now pure functions returning Uint8Array.
Re-exports preserve all public API paths. 213 tests pass unchanged.

- Replace inline import() type references with top-level type import in mock.ts
- Add _maxPosition parameter to isometricBuildValues for consistency with KinematicsProfile.buildValues signature
- Add comment explaining why TrainingMode.Idle maps to WEIGHT_TRAINING_PROFILE
- Simplify buildRepBoundary/buildSetBoundary byte-copy with Uint8Array.set()
- Document derivation of hardcoded sample counts in mode validation test
- Fix Prettier formatting and ESLint issues in mock.test.ts, kinematics.ts, notifications.ts

commit.message:
feat: LocalStack local Lambda, enum validation, agent-decided viz (#12)

* feat: add LocalStack local Lambda testing + fix asyncpg pool loop ownership

LocalStack setup (localstack/) lets developers run the Lambda locally
against local Postgres so MPI can test end-to-end without hitting dev RDS.
Zero application code changes for the LocalStack wiring — boto3 reads
AWS_ENDPOINT_URL natively.

Bug fixes found during local testing:

- connection.py: cached asyncpg pool was not scoped to its event loop.
  asyncio.run() creates a new loop per call, but the module-level _pool
  stayed bound to the old (closed) loop, causing "event loop is closed"
  on warm invocations. Fix: _pool_is_usable() checks pool._loop against
  the running loop; stale pools are terminate()'d, not just dropped.

- agent.py: get_agent() cached a single _agent regardless of model arg.
  Changing QUERY_MODEL had no effect until cold start. Fix: cache keyed
  by str(model).

- LLM_MODEL renamed to QUERY_MODEL everywhere (tests, docs, env examples)
  to match config.py and Terraform.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: schema config enum case mismatch + agent-decided viz handoff

LocalStack config had lowercase debit/credit but prod DB stores DEBIT/CREDIT,
causing silent 0-row returns. Added cold-start enum label validation in
schema_validator to catch mismatches early via pg_enum introspection.

Replaced handler-orchestrated automatic viz with agent-decided handoff using
Pydantic AI ToolOutput: agent chooses final_answer (text) or
final_answer_with_chart (requests viz), handler applies programmatic guardrails
(chartable tools, row count, time budget) before running viz agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Require root cause diagram to recursively expand to terminal causes

Each top-level cause in section 8.2 must now be recursively expanded
to concrete terminal causes (specific I/O ops, CPU work, kernel waits,
SDK/API calls), not stop at vague intermediate levels. Max depth 10.
Terminal cause = directly locatable code/system behavior that cannot
be further decomposed, or remaining duration <5ms.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

commit.message:
Fix page going black: remove global * transition selector

- Remove wildcard (*) CSS transition that caused all elements to
  transition colors during hydration
- Only apply transitions to specific container elements
- Extend no-transition timeout to 500ms for safer hydration

Root cause: Global * selector triggered massive style recalculation
that caused page to go black when theme state mismatched

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Pin Python to 3.11 to fix ChromaDB pydantic v1 compat on Render

ChromaDB >=0.5 uses pydantic v1 internally which fails on Python 3.14
with "unable to infer type for attribute chroma_server_nofile".
Python 3.11 is stable and supported by all dependencies.

commit.message:
feat: heartbeat polish — one-off datetime picker, history tab, enhanced /schedule command

**One-off Datetime Picker (schedules.js)**
- Added Recurring/One-time toggle buttons (line 314)
- Conditional rendering: time+days only for recurring, datetime-local only for one-off
- Updated formDataToScheduleDef() to handle one-off vs recurring payload
- Updated scheduleDefToFormData() to read runOnce/runAt from existing schedules
- Updated scheduleSave() validation: skip time/days for one-off, validate runAt instead

**History Tab (schedules.js)**
- Added _activeSubtab and _historyLog state variables
- Implemented renderHistoryTab() with execution log table (schedule, fired time, action, outcome)
- Added scheduleSetSubtab(name) to switch between Schedules and History tabs
- Added loadHistoryData() to fetch /api/schedules/log
- Outcome icons: ✓ (emerald/success), ✗ (red/error), — (gray/missed)
- Displays catch-up badge for missed executions

**Card Enhancements (schedules.js)**
- One-off schedules display 'Once' badge (amber) instead of days
- One-off display shows formatted datetime (e.g. 'Mar 6, 2:30 PM') instead of time
- For completed one-off tasks (enabled=false), show green '✓ Completed' instead of 'Run now'
- Updated renderScheduleCard() to detect isOneOff and render appropriately

**Enhanced /schedule Command**
- Rewritten .claude/commands/schedule.md with activation-instructions block
- Command now acts as interactive wizard instead of static documentation
- Guides user through: describe task → parse intent → build JSON → POST API → confirm
- Detects recurring vs one-off from natural language keywords
- Auto-generates ID from label, validates future datetime for one-off tasks
- Supports port discovery for Command Center server

**Protocol & Sync Updates**
- Updated ScheduleSyncPayload interface to include optional runOnce and runAt fields
- Updated getScheduleSync() in heartbeat.ts to include runOnce and runAt in sync payload
- Ensures UI can render all schedule attributes correctly on reconnect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Faster candle momentum — reduce lookback from 5→3, catch moves earlier
